step_by_step_thinking:
The child has cryptorchidism with an absent testicle on one side. The presence of a blind-end vessel during laparoscopy suggests that there might have been a vas deferens remnant or other vascular anomaly. Given this finding, it is prudent to proceed cautiously as these structures need careful dissection and removal if present. Considering the anatomical complexity revealed by the laparoscopic findings, performing open surgery through either inguinal (to explore further into the inguinal canal for possible missed ascent) or scrotal approaches would allow better visualization and management.

Answer: D: Inguinal approach To further investigate any potential remnants within the inguinal region where the testis should ascend normally, an inguinal approach seems indicated given its proximity to the area of concern.
